title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A richly symbolic novel exploring wealth, illusion, love, and moral decay in 1920s America, centered around Jay Gatsby’s obsessive pursuit of a lost past and the American Dream.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A classic fantasy tale following Bilbo Baggins as he is swept into an unexpected adventure involving dragons, treasure, and personal growth in a richly built mythical world.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: A fantasy coming-of-age story where a young boy discovers his magical identity and enters a hidden world of witches, wizards, friendship, and dark forces at Hogwarts.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A foundational software engineering guide that teaches principles, techniques, and mindset for writing clean, readable, maintainable, and professional-quality code.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt and David Thomas
description: A practical guide to software development emphasizing adaptable thinking, craftsmanship, problem-solving skills, and long-term professional growth for programmers.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A deeply human story addressing racial injustice, childhood innocence, and moral integrity through the eyes of a young girl in the segregated American South.

title: Introduction to Algorithms
author: Thomas H. Cormen
description: A comprehensive academic textbook that systematically explains fundamental algorithms, data structures, and computational problem-solving techniques used in computer science.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark science book that simplifies complex ideas about the universe, including black holes, time, space, and cosmology, for general readers.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian masterpiece depicting a future society ruled by surveillance, propaganda, and authoritarian control, exploring themes of truth, freedom, and oppression.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: An influential evolutionary biology work that reframes natural selection through the idea of genes as the primary units driving behavior, survival, and evolution.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping interdisciplinary narrative that traces the evolution of humankind from early Homo sapiens to modern civilization, exploring biology, anthropology, culture, economics, and the forces that shaped societies, beliefs, and global systems.

title: The Origin of Species
author: Charles Darwin
description: A groundbreaking scientific work that lays the foundation of evolutionary theory, explaining how natural selection drives the adaptation and diversification of life over long periods of time through observable biological variation.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical and allegorical novel that follows a young shepherd’s journey across deserts and cultures in search of personal destiny, exploring universal themes of purpose, intuition, spirituality, and self-discovery.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A deep exploration of human cognition that explains how two systems of thinking—fast intuitive reactions and slow analytical reasoning—shape judgment, decision-making, biases, and everyday behavior.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A practical and research-backed guide to behavior change that explains how small, consistent improvements compound over time to build effective habits, break harmful patterns, and achieve long-term personal growth.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
author: Mark Manson
description: A philosophical self-help book that challenges conventional positivity culture by emphasizing responsibility, values, and selective care, encouraging readers to focus only on what truly matters for a meaningful life.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: A productivity-focused guide that explores the ability to concentrate without distraction on cognitively demanding tasks, arguing that deep focus is a rare and valuable skill in an increasingly distracted world.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: A modern business methodology that promotes iterative product development, validated learning, and rapid experimentation to help startups and companies reduce risk and build successful, scalable products efficiently.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
author: Robert Kiyosaki
description: A personal finance book that contrasts two different financial mindsets through the stories of two father figures, teaching lessons on money management, investing, assets, and financial independence.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A powerful memoir recounting a journey from a strict and isolated upbringing in rural Idaho to achieving academic success, highlighting themes of resilience, identity, self-education, and transformation through learning.
